title: Reviewer Agent Output
description: JSON returned by each perspective reviewer sub-agent (maintainability, performance, quality, security). The orchestrator (pr-review SKILL) merges outputs from all four agents into a unified findings list. Distinct from skills/pr-review/feedback-schema.json, which describes the PR input data fetched from GitHub. Field names map directly to the findings table columns in the pr-review SKILL: Sev → severity, Source → source, File:Line → file + line, Finding → finding, Recommendation → recommendation, Effort → effort.

perspective.description: The review perspective this agent was instantiated for. Must match the value the agent is instructed to set (e.g. 'maintainability' for maintainability-reviewer).

summary.description: One-line verdict for the perspective mini-card shown in Step 6 of the pr-review skill. Use 'clean' when no findings, or a short description like '3 findings (1 CRITICAL, 2 MAJOR)'.

findings.description: Findings from this reviewer, ordered CRITICAL → MAJOR → MINOR → INFO.

severity.description: Impact level. Use the severity guidance defined in the reviewer agent's prompt (CRITICAL / MAJOR / MINOR). INFO is reserved for informational observations that do not require action.

source.description: Always 'agent' for findings produced by a reviewer sub-agent. When the orchestrator converts human review comments (reviews, review_comments, conversation_comments) into the same findings shape, it sets source to the GitHub login instead — that merged list is not validated against this schema.

file.description: Workspace-relative path to the file containing the issue (e.g. 'src/mypackage/core.py'). Use an empty string only when the finding is PR-level rather than tied to a specific file.

line.description: Line number within the file where the issue is located. Null when the finding is file-level rather than tied to a specific line.

finding.description: Concise description of the issue found. State what is wrong, not what to do about it — the recommendation field covers the fix.

recommendation.description: Actionable fix or improvement. Should be specific enough for the implementation-subagent to act on without further clarification.

effort.description: Estimated effort to fix the issue.
